Here-string guard

compound-command-validator: DENY → ASK for <<<

Why ASK, not a smarter block. A here-string can't be split into "safe receiver / dangerous receiver" statically — adversarial analysis found 9 ways to feed one to an interpreter that defeat any first-word check. So instead of hard-blocking the benign majority, it escalates: nothing runs without your confirmation.

Summary

Command → verdict

The 9 bypasses that killed the "smart block" idea
